name: Riverside Bank of Dublin
description_short: Riverside Bank of Dublin is a community bank offering personal and business banking solutions including checking, savings, lending, and online services with FDIC insurance.
description_long: Riverside Bank of Dublin is a community-focused bank headquartered in Dublin, Ohio, that has rapidly grown to earn recognition as the #8 fastest-growing private company in Central Ohio (2025 Fast 50 list) and Bank of the Year in the Under $500M Assets category by the Ohio Bankers League. The bank emphasizes personalized service and community banking expertise, with leadership that brings years of relationship-based banking experience. They offer comprehensive banking products for both personal and business customers, positioning themselves as an alternative to larger national banks by prioritizing direct customer relationships and local knowledge. The bank provides a full suite of personal banking products including checking, savings, money market, and certificate of deposit accounts, along with lending options such as personal loans, auto loans, student loans, credit cards, mortgage loans, and home equity lines of credit. For business customers, they offer commercial checking and savings accounts, business lines of credit, business credit cards, equipment financing, SBA loans, commercial real estate lending, and construction loans. All deposits are FDIC-insured, and they provide online and mobile banking platforms with bill pay, eAlerts, and mobile deposit features for both personal and business accounts. Riverside Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized service, noting that "a real person always answers the phone" and their 5-star rated customer service model. They highlight their use of the.BANK domain as a security measure and actively promote their community involvement and rapid growth trajectory. The bank is actively hiring, reflecting expansion momentum, and operates with a mission-driven approach to community banking. As a traditional community bank, Riverside Bank is best suited for customers seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making and personalized service rather than digital-first or purely online banking models. The primary caveat is that as a smaller regional bank, they may have fewer branch locations and less extensive product customization compared to large national banks, though their focus on personal relationships may offset this for community-oriented customers.
services: Money market and certificate of deposit (CD) accounts
Mortgage loans and home equity lines of credit
Business checking, savings, and money market accounts
Online banking and mobile banking apps
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Named #8 on Columbus Business First's 2025 Fast 50 list for rapid growth
Awarded Bank of the Year by Ohio Bankers League in Under $500M Assets category
Emphasizes personalized service with real people answering phones, 5-star rated customer experience
Uses .BANK domain for enhanced security and brand authenticity verification
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, and business lending options
Free online and mobile banking with 24/7 access, including mobile deposit and bill pay features
cons: Limited to Dublin/Central Ohio geographic footprint with no mention of multiple branch locations or national presence
No specific information provided about interest rates, APRs, or fee structures on website
Smaller regional bank may offer fewer specialized products or services compared to major national banks
best_for: Central Ohio residents and businesses seeking relationship-based community banking
Small to medium-sized business owners needing SBA loans, commercial real Estate, or equipment financing
Personal customers prioritizing personalized service and local decision-making over digital-only convenience
diagnosis: Riverside Bank of Dublin is best for Central Ohio residents and small-to-medium businesses that value personalized, relationship-based banking with local decision-making. The main caveat is their regional geographic limitation and smaller scale compared to national banks, which may limit product customization and branch accessibility for customers outside their service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Riverside Bank of Dublin offers personal and business banking including checking, savings, and online services with FDIC insurance.
